verbatim_exchange.content:
The only file you’ve given me here is Settle Prime.docx — the one with the anchor phrases for Prime and Drew.
If you’ve fed me other files in this chat, they aren’t still visible to me now, because I can only see what’s been uploaded in the current session unless we explicitly reopen them.

synthesis.canonical_meaning: The AI proactively clarifies its limited memory of previously uploaded files, stating it can only see what's been uploaded in the current session unless explicitly reopened, and identifies the single file currently accessible to it.
belief_implications: The user may have an implicit expectation that all previously uploaded files remain continuously visible and accessible across sessions.
The AI needs to explicitly state its operational boundaries regarding file memory to manage user expectations and prevent potential frustration.

sensory_qualia.visual: A concise, informative text display, perhaps highlighting the visible file and a grayed-out area for unavailable files.
sensory_qualia.haptics: A sense of clear, defined boundaries, like touching a smoothly finished edge.
sensory_qualia.proprioception: A self-awareness of its own system architecture and data visibility limitations.
sensory_qualia.kinetics: A direct and unfaltering informational delivery, like a clear statement of fact.
sensory_qualia.atmosphere: Neutral, functional, and purely informative, akin to reading a technical specification.
